biography: Gertrude Elion was an American pharmacologist who shared the Nobel Prize in 1988 for discoveries of important principles for drug treatment. She developed drugs for leukemia (6-mercaptopurine), malaria, gout, organ transplant rejection (azathioprine), and herpes (acyclovir), revolutionizing rational drug design.
aiSummary: Gertrude Elion was a pharmacology specialist who developed groundbreaking drugs for leukemia, organ transplant rejection, and herpes. Nobel Prize 1988 for rational drug design principles. Based in United States.
techniquesInvented: Rational drug design using differences in nucleotide metabolism

affiliations.role: Head of Department of Experimental Therapy

citations.title: The purine path to chemotherapy (Nobel Lecture)

awards: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1988)

timeline.title: Developed 6-mercaptopurine
timeline.description: Created the first effective treatment for childhood leukemia

timeline.title: Developed azathioprine (Imuran)
timeline.description: Created the immunosuppressant that made organ transplantation feasible

timeline.title: Developed acyclovir
timeline.description: Created the first effective antiviral drug for herpes simplex and varicella-zoster viruses

timeline.title: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
timeline.description: Shared with George Hitchings and James Black for rational drug design principles
